args.query: site:docs.stripe.com webhooks signature verification troubleshooting endpoint secret raw body livemode

content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Resolve webhook signature verification errors | Stripe Documentation

  - url: https://docs.stripe.com/webhooks/signature

  - source: Stripe Documentation

  - domain: docs.stripe.com


  ## Content


  # Resolve webhook signature verification errors


  Stripe recommends verifying webhook events with an official library. Signature verification
  uses three inputs: the exact request body sent by Stripe, the `Stripe-Signature`
  request header, and the endpoint secret associated with the webhook endpoint. If
  verification reports that no signatures match the expected signature for the payload,
  at least one of these inputs is incorrect.


  ## Check the endpoint secret


  The endpoint secret must correspond to the source that delivered the event. A webhook
  endpoint managed in the Stripe Dashboard or Workbench has its own endpoint secret.
  A Stripe CLI forwarding session prints a different secret when `stripe listen` starts.
  Both values use the `whsec_` prefix, but they are not interchangeable.


  ## Check the request body


  The verification function must receive the unmodified UTF-8 request body. Parsing
  or reserializing JSON, changing whitespace or key order, or changing the encoding
  can cause signature verification to fail. Framework and middleware configuration
  can affect whether the original request body remains available to the verification
  code.


  The webhook payload contains a Stripe Event object, whose `id` identifies the event.
  The documentation examples show event identifiers using the `evt_...` form.


  ## Check the signature value


  The signature input is taken from the `Stripe-Signature` header. Stripe recommends
  checking that the application extracts the expected header and passes it to the
  verification function. Signature values include a timestamp and one or more signature
  components.


  Troubleshooting of the endpoint secret, raw request body, and signature header is
  performed in the developer''s Dashboard, CLI, and application environment.

  '

content.content:
The connector’s current failure is a **Stripe webhook signature-verification error**: `no_matching_signatures_for_payload`. Stripe says this usually means the endpoint secret, unmodified request body, or `Stripe-Signature` header is incorrect.

Please send this **redacted diagnostic summary**:

```text
Stripe event ID: evt_... (if available)
Webhook endpoint identifier: <name or ID; do not send the signing secret>
Environment: live/test
Last delivery timestamp: <timestamp and timezone>
HTTP status returned by our webhook handler: <code>
Redacted handler error text/response body: <text>

Original UTF-8 request body reaches signature verification unchanged: yes/no/unknown
Stripe-Signature header reaches the verifier: yes/no/unknown
Endpoint secret was recently rotated: yes/no/unknown
Secret is from this Stripe Dashboard/Workbench endpoint, not Stripe CLI: yes/no/unknown
Endpoint and configured secret are both in the same mode: yes/no/unknown
```

**Do not send:**

- The `whsec_...` signing secret
- API keys, passwords, or tokens
- The raw webhook payload
- Full production logs or customer/payment data
- The complete `Stripe-Signature` header; a yes/no confirmation is sufficient

The most common causes are using a CLI forwarding secret for a Dashboard endpoint, using a test-mode secret in live mode, or parsing/re-serializing the request body before signature verification. Stripe’s reference: [Resolve webhook signature verification errors](https://docs.stripe.com/webhooks/signature).

final_answer:
The connector’s current failure is a **Stripe webhook signature-verification error**: `no_matching_signatures_for_payload`. Stripe says this usually means the endpoint secret, unmodified request body, or `Stripe-Signature` header is incorrect.

Please send this **redacted diagnostic summary**:

```text
Stripe event ID: evt_... (if available)
Webhook endpoint identifier: <name or ID; do not send the signing secret>
Environment: live/test
Last delivery timestamp: <timestamp and timezone>
HTTP status returned by our webhook handler: <code>
Redacted handler error text/response body: <text>

Original UTF-8 request body reaches signature verification unchanged: yes/no/unknown
Stripe-Signature header reaches the verifier: yes/no/unknown
Endpoint secret was recently rotated: yes/no/unknown
Secret is from this Stripe Dashboard/Workbench endpoint, not Stripe CLI: yes/no/unknown
Endpoint and configured secret are both in the same mode: yes/no/unknown
```

**Do not send:**

- The `whsec_...` signing secret
- API keys, passwords, or tokens
- The raw webhook payload
- Full production logs or customer/payment data
- The complete `Stripe-Signature` header; a yes/no confirmation is sufficient

The most common causes are using a CLI forwarding secret for a Dashboard endpoint, using a test-mode secret in live mode, or parsing/re-serializing the request body before signature verification. Stripe’s reference: [Resolve webhook signature verification errors](https://docs.stripe.com/webhooks/signature).
